106-24-1.name: Geraniol
106-24-1.notes: Primary rose note. Found in rose, geranium, citronella. IFRA: sensitizer, restricted to ~1% in leave-on.

106-22-9.name: Citronellol
106-22-9.notes: Fresh rose with citrus facet. Key component of rose and geranium oils.

60-12-8.name: Phenethyl Alcohol
60-12-8.notes: The 'rose petal' molecule. Major component of rose oil (60%+). Very versatile.

122-78-1.name: Phenylacetaldehyde
122-78-1.notes: Hyacinth and narcissus character. Very powerful. Needs careful dosing.

107-75-5.name: Hydroxycitronellal
107-75-5.notes: THE muguet/lily-of-the-valley molecule. IFRA: sensitizer, restricted. Replaced by Lilial (now also banned) and Lyral (also restricted). Bourgeonal and cyclamen aldehyde are current alternatives.

80-54-6.name: Lilial (Butylphenyl Methylpropional)
80-54-6.notes: BANNED in EU/UK from March 2022 (CMR Cat 1B). Was the primary muguet replacement for hydroxycitronellal. Now replaced by Bourgeonal, cyclamen aldehyde, Florol.

31906-04-4.name: Lyral (Hydroxyisohexyl 3-Cyclohexene Carboxaldehyde)
31906-04-4.notes: Muguet/lily note. IFRA restricted (sensitizer). Less fresh than Lilial, more long-lasting.

18127-01-0.name: Bourgeonal
18127-01-0.notes: Lilial replacement. Fresh muguet with green facets. Used post-Lilial ban.

63500-88-9.name: Florol (Tetrahydro-2-isobutyl-4-methyl-4(2H)-pyranol)
63500-88-9.notes: Firmenich muguet alternative. Non-sensitizing. Natural-smelling floral.

103-95-7.name: Cyclamen Aldehyde
103-95-7.notes: Cyclamen flower note. Green-floral. Lilial alternative.

127-41-3.name: Ionone Alpha
127-41-3.notes: Classic violet note. Discovered 1893. Woody-floral fade.

14901-07-6.name: Ionone Beta
14901-07-6.notes: More raspberry than alpha-ionone. The 'violet-raspberry' molecule.

79-76-5.name: Methyl Ionone Gamma
79-76-5.notes: The finest of the ionones. Warm orris-violet. Essential in iris accords.

79-69-6.name: Irone Alpha
79-69-6.notes: The genuine orris/iris molecule. Found naturally in orris butter. Extremely expensive. Synthetic alpha-irone is the key to orris reconstructions.

8022-96-6.name: Jasmine Absolute (Natural)
8022-96-6.notes: Queen of florals. ~5000 flowers for 1g. Contains benzyl acetate, linalool, indole, cis-jasmone, methyl anthranilate.

488-49-3.name: Cis-Jasmone
488-49-3.notes: Key jasmine character. Natural-smelling jasmine with herbal undertones.

140-11-4.name: Benzyl Acetate
140-11-4.notes: Fresh jasmine top note. The most jasmine-like single chemical. Very common.

120-72-9.name: Indole
120-72-9.notes: The 'indolic' backbone of jasmine and orange blossom. Fecal in overdose, floral at trace. Essential for realistic jasmine.

24851-98-7.name: Hedione (Methyl Dihydrojasmonate)
24851-98-7.notes: Revolutionary. Introduced in Eau Sauvage (1966). Adds 'radiance' and diffusivity. Used in virtually every modern fragrance. Not a true jasmine—more of a 'jasmine impression' enhancer.

8016-38-4.name: Neroli Oil (Natural)
8016-38-4.notes: Steam-distilled orange blossom. Classic Eau de Cologne ingredient. Contains linalool, linalyl acetate, limonene.

134-20-3.name: Methyl Anthranilate
134-20-3.notes: Orange blossom and Concord grape character. Key in neroli accords. Reacts with aldehydes (Schiff base formation).

8024-05-3.name: Tuberose Absolute (Natural)
8024-05-3.notes: Crepuscular, narcotic floral. Contains methyl salicylate, methyl anthranilate, eugenol. One of the most expensive naturals.

8006-81-3.name: Ylang Ylang Oil (Natural)
8006-81-3.notes: Cananga odorata. Sweet floral with banana facets. Extra grade is finest. Key in Chanel No. 5 and oriental blends.

97-53-0.name: Eugenol
97-53-0.notes: Clove and carnation character. IFRA restricted (sensitizer). Found in clove oil, cinnamon leaf, ylang-ylang.

97-54-1.name: Iso Eugenol
97-54-1.notes: Smoother, more floral than eugenol. Key carnation material. IFRA restricted.

16409-43-1.name: Rose Oxide
16409-43-1.notes: The 'rose top note sparkle' molecule. Trace amounts in rose oil. Extremely powerful—used at <0.1%.

23726-93-4.name: Damascenone
23726-93-4.notes: Key rose character. Plum-tobacco-rose. Found in rose oil and red fruits. Extremely potent.

23726-92-3.name: Damascone Beta
23726-92-3.notes: Rose with plum and cassis facets. More fruity than damascenone. Key in modern rose accords.

8013-49-8.name: Narcissus Absolute (Natural)
8013-49-8.notes: Green-floral with animalic depth. Rare and expensive. Contains benzyl acetate, methyl benzoate, indole.

111-12-6.name: Gardenia Acetal (Methyl 2-Octynoate)
111-12-6.notes: Gardenia and violet leaf character. Very powerful. Used at trace levels.

8007-75-8.name: Bergamot Oil FCF (Natural)
8007-75-8.notes: The backbone of Eau de Cologne. FCF = furanocoumarin-free (required by IFRA for phototoxicity). Contains linalyl acetate (35%+), limonene, linalool.

5989-27-5.name: Limonene
5989-27-5.notes: The most common terpene. 90%+ of orange oil. Very short-lived. Weak odor but essential for 'juice' effect.

5392-40-5.name: Citral
5392-40-5.notes: Intense lemon. Mixture of neral and geranial. IFRA: sensitizer. Key in lemon reconstructions.

78-70-6.name: Linalool
78-70-6.notes: Universal building block. Found in 200+ essential oils. Fresh floral-citrus. IFRA: sensitizer but widely used. Key in lavender, bergamot, rose accords.

115-95-7.name: Linalyl Acetate
115-95-7.notes: Bergamot and lavender character. Main component of bergamot oil (35%+). Smoother than linalool.

4674-50-4.name: Nootkatone
4674-50-4.notes: The signature grapefruit molecule. Very expensive natural; bio-synthesized versions available. Woody-grapefruit.

8014-17-3.name: Petitgrain Oil Paraguay (Natural)
8014-17-3.notes: Bitter orange leaf distillation. Fresh citrus-woody. Key in cologne and fougère accords.

112-31-2.name: Decanal (Aldehyde C-10)
112-31-2.notes: Orange peel character. Key aldehyde in Chanel No. 5. Use at trace for sparkle.

112-54-9.name: Dodecanal (Aldehyde C-12 MNA)
112-54-9.notes: THE Chanel No. 5 aldehyde. Soapy, metallic, fatty. Use at <1% for 'champagne fizz'.

112-44-7.name: Undecanal (Aldehyde C-11)
112-44-7.notes: Floral-aldehydic. 'Rose-aldehyde' character. Important in aldehydic florals.

124-19-6.name: Nonanal (Aldehyde C-9)
124-19-6.notes: Rose-citrus aldehyde. Natural component of rose and citrus oils.

5442-12-2.name: Iso E Super (Tetramethyl Octahydronaphthalenfuran)
5442-12-2.notes: The molecule that launched molecular perfumery. Star of Molecule 01. Velvety woody-amber. Enhances other materials. Can be used at 50%+ of formula.

6790-58-5.name: Ambroxan (Ambroxide)
6790-58-5.notes: Synthetic ambergris. Key in Sauvage, Baccarat Rouge 540. Very long-lasting. Warm-woody-ambergris. Can be used as a near-solo ingredient (Not A Perfume, JHAG).

70788-30-6.name: Norlimbanol (Timberol)
70788-30-6.notes: The 'dry wood' molecule. Key in Dior Sauvage and modern masculines. Amber-woody-dry. Very tenacious.

8006-87-9.name: Sandalwood Oil Indian (Natural)
8006-87-9.notes: Santalum album. The finest sandalwood. Endangered/overharvested. Contains alpha-santalol (50%+) and beta-santalol. Australian (S. spicatum) is sustainable alternative with different profile.

91481-32-0.name: Ebanol
91481-32-0.notes: Synthetic sandalwood. Creamy, sweet, woody. Stronger than sandela. Good sandalwood reconstruction ingredient.

1205-09-0.name: Javanol
1205-09-0.notes: Firmenich. The most potent synthetic sandalwood. 5-10x stronger than Ebanol. Creamy-sweet-woody.

8000-27-9.name: Patchouli Oil (Natural)
8000-27-9.notes: Essential to chypre and oriental families. Contains patchoulol (27-35%). Heart (distilled) version is smoother. Indonesian is standard; premium grades from Sulawesi.

8016-96-4.name: Vetiver Oil Haitian (Natural)
8016-96-4.notes: Haitian is smoother, more grapefruit. Java is smokier. Bourbon (Réunion) is finest. Contains vetiverol, vetivone. Essential in masculine and chypre.

62563-80-8.name: Vetiveryl Acetate
62563-80-8.notes: Acetylated vetiver. Smoother and more elegant than vetiver oil. Less earthy, more refined woody-grapefruit.

77-53-2.name: Cedrol
77-53-2.notes: Cedar alcohol. Dry, warm cedar character. Fixative.

33704-61-9.name: Cashmeran
33704-61-9.notes: The 'cashmere sweater' molecule. Woody-musky-powdery. Used in clean-laundry and soft-woody accords. Key in detergent perfumery too.

64430-87-5.name: Sandalore
64430-87-5.notes: Sandalwood-musk hybrid. Not as creamy as Ebanol or Javanol but adds musky warmth.

107898-32-0.name: Polysantol
107898-32-0.notes: IFF. Creamy sandalwood with floral nuance. One of the best modern sandalwood synthetics.

8016-26-0.name: Labdanum Absolute (Natural)
8016-26-0.notes: Cistus ladanifer. The backbone of amber accords. Contains labdane diterpenes, ambrarome. Provides the 'warm amber' foundation.

121-33-5.name: Vanillin
121-33-5.notes: Synthesized 1874. The most important gourmand molecule. Key in oriental/ambery and gourmand families. Found naturally in vanilla beans (1-3%).

121-32-4.name: Ethyl Vanillin
121-32-4.notes: 2-3x stronger than vanillin. Sweeter, more intense. Used when vanillin isn't sweet enough.

9000-72-0.name: Benzoin Resinoid (Natural)
9000-72-0.notes: Styrax benzoin or tonkinensis. Sweet balsamic-vanilla. Key in oriental and amber accords. Contains benzoic acid, vanillin, coniferyl benzoate.

9000-64-0.name: Tolu Balsam (Natural)
9000-64-0.notes: Myroxylon balsamum. Cinnamon-vanilla-balsamic. Used in oriental and amber accords.

8016-37-3.name: Myrrh Resinoid (Natural)
8016-37-3.notes: Commiphora myrrha. Warm, balsamic, slightly medicinal. Used in incense and oriental accords.

8016-36-2.name: Frankincense Oil (Natural)
8016-36-2.notes: Boswellia species. Lemony-pine-resinous top note. Used in meditation and religious ceremony for millennia.

28645-51-4.name: Ambrettolide
28645-51-4.notes: Natural musk from ambrette seed. Macrocyclic musk. Fruity-floral-musk. One of the few 'natural' musks. Very expensive; synthetic version widely used.

1222-05-5.name: Galaxolide (HHCB)
1222-05-5.notes: Most-used musk in perfumery. Clean laundry/skin musk. Polycyclic. Environmental concerns (bioaccumulation). Used in everything from fine fragrance to detergent.

94201-28-6.name: Habanolide
94201-28-6.notes: Warm skin musk with metallic edge. Macrocyclic (more biodegradable than polycyclic). Warmer than Galaxolide. Used in modern 'skin scent' compositions.

105-96-4.name: Ethylene Brassylate
105-96-4.notes: Macrocylic musk. Clean-powdery. Good fixative. Often used as the musk backbone in masculine fragrances.

106-02-5.name: Exaltolide
106-02-5.notes: Pentadecanolide. The classic macrocyclic musk. Elegant, warm. Natural musk-like. More expensive than Galaxolide but higher quality.

81-15-2.name: Musk T (2,4,6-Trinitro-1,3-dimethyl-tert-butylbenzene)
81-15-2.notes: HISTORICAL. Nitro musk. Now banned/restricted due to environmental persistence. Replaced by polycyclic and macrocyclic musks.

141776-68-7.name: Serenolide
141776-68-7.notes: IFF. Light, clean musk with floral undertones. Good for fresh-clean compositions.

141712-42-3.name: Sylkolide
141712-42-3.notes: Symrise. Soft skin musk. Clean, powdery. Good alternative to Galaxolide with better biodegradability.

104-55-2.name: Cinnamaldehyde
104-55-2.notes: Cinnamon character. IFRA restricted (sensitizer). Very powerful. Key in oriental and gourmand accords.

104-54-1.name: Cinnamyl Alcohol
104-54-1.notes: Softer, more floral than cinnamaldehyde. Hyacinth and balsamic character.

8000-66-6.name: Cardamom Oil (Natural)
8000-66-6.notes: Elettaria cardamomum. Fresh spicy-eucalyptus. Contains 1,8-cineole, alpha-terpinyl acetate. Key in oriental and fougère.

8006-82-4.name: Black Pepper Oil (Natural)
8006-82-4.notes: Piper nigrum. Warm, sharp, woody-spicy. Contains beta-caryophyllene, limonene. Key in modern masculine fragrances.

8007-08-7.name: Ginger Oil (Natural)
8007-08-7.notes: Zingiber officinale. Fresh-spicy with citrus-woody facets. Contains zingiberene, curcumene.

8023-86-3.name: Galbanum Oil (Natural)
8023-86-3.notes: Ferula galbaniflua. THE green material. Bitter-green, sharp. Contains alpha-pinene, beta-pinene. Key in Vent Vert and green florals.

928-96-1.name: Leaf Alcohol (cis-3-Hexenol)
928-96-1.notes: The 'freshly cut grass' molecule. Found in green leaves. Very powerful. Essential for green accords.

33704-76-0.name: Stemone
33704-76-0.notes: Fig leaf and tomato stem character. Key in modern green-fig compositions.

8024-08-6.name: Violet Leaf Absolute (Natural)
8024-08-6.notes: Viola odorata. Green-cucumber-peppery. Contains 2,6-nonadienal (cucumber aldehyde). Expensive but irreplaceable.

4940-11-8.name: Ethyl Maltol
4940-11-8.notes: 3-5x sweeter than maltol. THE gourmand molecule. Pioneered by Angel (1992). Cotton candy-strawberry-caramel. Extremely sweet.

118-71-8.name: Maltol
118-71-8.notes: Natural caramel note. Found in strawberry, mango, roasted coffee. Less sweet than ethyl maltol but more natural-smelling.

104-67-6.name: Gamma-Undecalactone (Aldehyde C-14)
104-67-6.notes: THE peach molecule. Despite 'aldehyde' name, it's a lactone. Creamy-fruity-peach. Key in peach, apricot, and fruity-floral accords.

104-61-0.name: Gamma-Nonalactone (Aldehyde C-18)
104-61-0.notes: THE coconut molecule. Creamy-sweet-coconut. Used in suntan-lotion and tropical accords.

3658-77-3.name: Furaneol (Strawberry Furanone)
3658-77-3.notes: Strawberry-tropical character. Found in strawberries, mangoes, pineapple. Key in fruity and tropical accords.

6413-10-1.name: Fructone
6413-10-1.notes: Fresh apple-fruity. Light, clean fruity character.

67634-00-8.name: Allyl Amyl Glycolate
67634-00-8.notes: Pineapple-green character. Very diffusive. Used in tropical and fruity accords.

8024-06-4.name: Tonka Bean Absolute (Natural)
8024-06-4.notes: Dipteryx odorata. Contains coumarin (30-50%). Sweet-hay-almond-vanilla. Key in fougère and oriental. IFRA: coumarin restricted.

91-64-5.name: Coumarin
91-64-5.notes: Synthesized 1868. Key in Fougère Royale (1882). Sweet-hay-tonka. IFRA restricted. Backbone of the fougère family.

119-84-6.name: Dihydrocoumarin
119-84-6.notes: Smoother, more coconut-like than coumarin. Tonka-coconut sweetness.

84649-99-0.name: Cocoa Absolute (Natural)
84649-99-0.notes: Theobroma cacao. Rich bitter chocolate. Used in gourmand accords. Very tenacious.

8001-67-0.name: Coffee Absolute (Natural)
8001-67-0.notes: Coffea arabica. Rich, roasted, bitter. Used in gourmand and oriental accords.

60753-08-8.name: Calone (Methylbenzodioxepinone)
60753-08-8.notes: THE sea breeze molecule. Invented 1966, popularized 1990s. Key to Cool Water (1988) and the entire aquatic family. Very powerful.

18479-58-8.name: Dihydromyrcenol
18479-58-8.notes: The Cool Water molecule. Fresh-citrus-lavender-metallic. Revolutionized masculine perfumery (1988). Key in aquatic and fresh fragrances.

67634-15-5.name: Floralozone
67634-15-5.notes: Ozone-fresh character. Clean-aquatic-floral. Used in modern fresh fragrances.

8000-28-0.name: Lavender Oil (Natural)
8000-28-0.notes: Lavandula angustifolia. THE fougère ingredient. Contains linalool (35%+), linalyl acetate (25%+). Top note of the masculine fragrance tradition.

8000-25-9.name: Rosemary Oil (Natural)
8000-25-9.notes: Contains 1,8-cineole, camphor, alpha-pinene. Key in Eau de Cologne and fougère.

8000-48-4.name: Eucalyptus Oil (Natural)
8000-48-4.notes: Contains 1,8-cineole (80%+). Fresh-medicinal-cool. Used in respiratory and fresh accords.

470-82-6.name: 1,8-Cineole (Eucalyptol)
470-82-6.notes: Fresh-camphoraceous. Found in eucalyptus, rosemary, cardamom. The 'cool breath' molecule.

8016-63-5.name: Clary Sage Oil (Natural)
8016-63-5.notes: Contains linalyl acetate, sclareol. Herbal-amber-tea. Source of sclareol for Ambroxan synthesis.

8002-73-1.name: Orris Concrete/Butter (Natural)
8002-73-1.notes: Iris pallida rhizomes. Must age 3-5 years. Contains alpha-irone. The most expensive raw material per gram. Buttery-powdery-woody-violet.

120-57-0.name: Heliotropin (Piperonal)
120-57-0.notes: Heliotrope and cherry-almond character. Sweet-powdery-floral. Key in powdery and oriental accords.

98-86-2.name: Acetophenone
98-86-2.notes: Hawthorn and orange blossom character. Sweet-floral-powdery.

93-19-6.name: Isobutyl Quinoline (IBQ)
93-19-6.notes: THE leather molecule. Earthy-leather-nutty. Key in Cuir de Russie, Knize Ten. Very powerful and tenacious.

8001-88-5.name: Birch Tar Oil (Natural)
8001-88-5.notes: Betula alba. Smoky-leather-tar. Traditional Russian leather perfume ingredient. Contains betulin, guaiacol. IFRA: restricted (polycyclic aromatics).

110-41-8.name: Aldehyde C-12 MOF (2-Methyl Undecanal)
110-41-8.notes: Metallic-amber aldehyde. Key in Chanel No. 5 and aldehydic florals. Use at trace.

124-13-0.name: Aldehyde C-8 (Octanal)
124-13-0.notes: Fatty-orange aldehyde. Used in citrus reconstructions for 'juice' effect.

77-83-8.name: Aldehyde C-16 (Strawberry Aldehyde, Ethyl Methyl Phenylglycidate)
77-83-8.notes: Despite 'aldehyde' name, it's a strawberry note. Not actually an aldehyde chemically. Key in strawberry reconstructions.

9000-50-4.name: Oakmoss Absolute (Natural)
9000-50-4.notes: Evernia prunastri. THE chypre ingredient. IFRA severely restricted (sensitizer, atranyl aldehydes). Synthetic replacement: Evernyl. Devastated the chypre family.

4707-47-5.name: Evernyl (Veramoss)
4707-47-5.notes: Synthetic oakmoss replacement. IFRA-compliant. Less rich than natural oakmoss but essential for modern chypre.

19700-21-1.name: Geosmin
19700-21-1.notes: The 'rain on dry earth' molecule (petrichor). Extremely powerful—used at parts-per-trillion. Found in beetroot. Key in earthy and mineral accords.

68129-81-7.name: Vetiverol
68129-81-7.notes: Primary alcohol from vetiver oil. Earthy-woody-rooty.

5986-55-0.name: Patchoulol
5986-55-0.notes: Main component of patchouli oil (27-35%). Earthy-woody-sweet. Can be synthesized via biotech.

8007-01-0.name: Rose Otto (Natural)
8007-01-0.notes: Rosa damascena. Steam-distilled. ~60,000 roses for 30ml. Contains citronellol (50%+), geraniol (20%+), nerol, phenethyl alcohol, rose oxide, damascenone.

72968-50-4.name: Orange Blossom Absolute (Natural)
72968-50-4.notes: Citrus aurantium flowers. Solvent-extracted. Richer and more indolic than neroli. Contains methyl anthranilate, indole, linalool.

8022-98-6.name: Mimosa Absolute (Natural)
8022-98-6.notes: Acacia dealbata. Powdery-green-honey floral. Used in floral bouquets and powdery accords.

8015-62-1.name: Ambrette Seed Absolute (Natural)
8015-62-1.notes: Hibiscus abelmoschus. The only natural plant musk. Contains ambrettolide. Very expensive. Fruity-floral-musk.

8021-36-1.name: Opoponax Resinoid (Natural)
8021-36-1.notes: Commiphora erythraea. Sweet balsamic-amber. Key in oriental and amber accords.

8023-89-0.name: Elemi Resinoid (Natural)
8023-89-0.notes: Canarium luzonicum. Fresh-lemony-balsamic. Bridge between citrus top and incense base. Used in incense accords.

89997-74-0.name: Cistus Labdanum Oil (Natural)
89997-74-0.notes: Steam-distilled Cistus ladanifer. More ambery than labdanum absolute. Rich warm amber. Key in amber and chypre accords.

8012-89-3.name: Beeswax Absolute (Natural)
8012-89-3.notes: Apis mellifera wax. Honey-wax-warm. Adds warmth and honey nuances. Very tenacious.

27710-82-3.name: Koavone (Methyl Lavender Ketone)
27710-82-3.notes: Lavender-fruity-floral. Used in fougère and floral-herbal accords.

56973-85-4.name: Dynascone 10
56973-85-4.notes: Firmenich. Galbanum-green with fruity nuance. Modern galbanum alternative.

502-72-7.name: Exaltone (Cyclopentadecanone)
502-72-7.notes: Macrocyclic musk ketone. Warm-animalic musk. More animalic than Exaltolide.

68039-48-5.name: Triplal (2,4-Dimethyl-3-Cyclohexene Carboxaldehyde)
68039-48-5.notes: Intense green-leafy character. Very powerful. Key in green and herbal accords.

89-78-1.name: Menthol
89-78-1.notes: Cool-minty. TRPM8 receptor activator (cold sensation). Key in mint and cooling accords.

99-49-0.name: Carvone
99-49-0.notes: Spearmint (l-carvone) or caraway (d-carvone). Enantiomers smell different. Key in mint and spice accords.

104-46-1.name: Anethole
104-46-1.notes: Anise and licorice character. Found in anise, fennel, star anise. Key in pastis and anisic accords.

8014-13-9.name: Cumin Oil (Natural)
8014-13-9.notes: Cuminum cyminum. Warm-spicy-sweaty. Contains cuminaldehyde. Key in oriental and exotic spice accords.

564-20-5.name: Sclareolide
564-20-5.notes: Amber-tobacco character. Semi-synthetic from clary sage. Bridge to Ambroxan synthesis.

106-25-2.name: Nerol
106-25-2.notes: Fresh rose-citrus. Cis isomer of geraniol. Found in neroli, rose, lemon. Softer and fresher than geraniol.

6812-78-8.name: Rhodinol
6812-78-8.notes: Enriched citronellol fraction. The finest rose alcohol. Sweeter, more floral than standard citronellol.

100-86-7.name: Dimethyl Benzyl Carbinol (DMBC)
100-86-7.notes: Lilac and fresh floral character. Key in lilac and muguet accords.

5462-06-2.name: Canthoxal
5462-06-2.notes: Hawthorn blossom with anisic sweetness. Key in hawthorn and sweet floral accords.

10371-08-3.name: Majantol
10371-08-3.notes: Muguet-lily with clean sweetness. Lilial alternative. Non-sensitizing.

125109-89-3.name: Florhydral
125109-89-3.notes: Fresh muguet with watery-green facets. Modern lily of the valley ingredient.

93-58-3.name: Methyl Benzoate
93-58-3.notes: Ylang-ylang and fruity-floral character. Found in ylang-ylang and tuberose.

7212-44-4.name: Nerolidol
7212-44-4.notes: Floral-woody with apple-rose nuance. Found in neroli, rose, and many flowers. Good fixative.

4602-84-0.name: Farnesol
4602-84-0.notes: Linden blossom character. Mild, sweet, green-floral. Found in linden, rose, neroli. IFRA: sensitizer.

1365-19-1.name: Linalool Oxide
1365-19-1.notes: Floral-woody with earthy nuance. Found in tea, grape, passion fruit.

104-27-8.name: Methoxyphenylbutanone
104-27-8.notes: Mimosa and sweet floral character. Key in mimosa and sweet floral accords.

432-25-7.name: Cyclal C
432-25-7.notes: Linden blossom and fresh-green-floral. Key in linden and fresh floral accords.

105-14-6.name: Mefrosol
105-14-6.notes: Linden/honey flower. Fresh-sweet-floral. Key in linden and honey-floral accords.

123-11-5.name: Anisic Aldehyde
123-11-5.notes: Hawthorn blossom with anisic sweetness. Powdery-floral. Very versatile.

72926-33-5.name: Tetrahydromuguol
72926-33-5.notes: Muguet-lily of the valley. Non-sensitizing alternative to Lilial and Lyral.

94133-92-3.name: Peony Nitrile
94133-92-3.notes: Peony flower character. Fresh-green-rosy. Nitrile-based (stable in bleach).

72401-54-8.name: Rose Petal Nitrile
72401-54-8.notes: Green-metallic rose character. More diffusive than geraniol. Used in fresh rose accords.

101-48-8.name: Phenylacetaldehyde Dimethyl Acetal
101-48-8.notes: Stable form of phenylacetaldehyde. Hyacinth-green floral. Longer-lasting than the aldehyde.

93963-40-7.name: Agrumen Nitrile
93963-40-7.notes: Citrus-floral nitrile. Stable in functional products. Fresh lemon-green.

10030-28-9.name: Oxane
10030-28-9.notes: Fresh rose-green character. Adds sparkle to rose accords.

80-56-8.name: Alpha-Pinene
80-56-8.notes: Most abundant terpene in nature. Pine-citrus-fresh. Found in pine, rosemary, basil.

127-91-3.name: Beta-Pinene
127-91-3.notes: Dry pine-herbal. Less sharp than alpha-pinene. Found in many essential oils.

123-35-3.name: Myrcene
123-35-3.notes: Tropical-citrus-fruity. Found in bay, lemongrass, hop, mango. Key in tropical accords.

13877-91-3.name: Ocimene
13877-91-3.notes: Tropical-green-citrus. Found in basil, mint, mango. Very diffusive.

586-62-9.name: Terpinolene
586-62-9.notes: Fresh pine-citrus-herbal. Found in tea tree, cumin, apple. Fresh top note.

80-26-2.name: Alpha-Terpinyl Acetate
80-26-2.notes: Bergamot-lavender character. Key component of cardamom oil. Fresh fruity-herbal.

106-23-0.name: Citronellal
106-23-0.notes: Intense lemon-citronella. Key in citronella oil and lemon-scented tea tree.

8008-26-2.name: Lime Oil Distilled (Natural)
8008-26-2.notes: Citrus aurantifolia. Sharp-tart lime. Distilled version loses some freshness but is phototoxicity-safe.

8008-56-8.name: Lemon Oil (Natural)
8008-56-8.notes: Citrus limon. Bright zesty lemon. Contains citral, limonene, pinenes. Cold-pressed preferred.

8008-57-9.name: Sweet Orange Oil (Natural)
8008-57-9.notes: Citrus sinensis. Sweet juicy orange. 90%+ limonene. The most-produced citrus oil.

8016-20-4.name: Grapefruit Oil (Natural)
8016-20-4.notes: Citrus paradisi. Bitter-fresh grapefruit. Contains nootkatone. IFRA: phototoxic (furanocoumarins).

8008-31-9.name: Mandarin Oil (Natural)
8008-31-9.notes: Citrus reticulata. Sweetest citrus oil. Contains methyl N-methyl anthranilate (unique to mandarin).

8000-29-1.name: Citronella Oil (Natural)
8000-29-1.notes: Cymbopogon nardus. Lemon-grassy. Contains citronellal, geraniol, citronellol. Insect repellent.

98-55-5.name: Terpineol
98-55-5.notes: Lilac-pine-floral. Widely used in soap perfumery. Clean-fresh character.

3387-41-5.name: Sabinene
3387-41-5.notes: Pepper-citrus-woody. Found in black pepper, juniper, tea tree.

99-83-2.name: Phellandrene Alpha
99-83-2.notes: Pepper-herbal-citrus. Found in eucalyptus, pepper, parsley.

122681-41-0.name: Georgywood
122681-41-0.notes: IFF. Cedar-vetiver-amber hybrid. Dry woody character. Key in modern woody-amber fragrances.

6280-33-5.name: Guaiaconol
6280-33-5.notes: Guaiac-smoky-woody. Key in tea and smoky accords.

8023-11-2.name: Guaiacwood Oil (Natural)
8023-11-2.notes: Bulnesia sarmientoi. Tea-like woody-rose. Overharvested—sustainability concerns. Contains guaiol, bulnesol.

8015-65-4.name: Amyris Oil (Natural)
8015-65-4.notes: Amyris balsamifera. Often called 'West Indian Sandalwood' but unrelated. Sweet woody-balsamic.

90-05-1.name: Guaiacol
90-05-1.notes: Smoky-woody-phenolic. Found in roasted coffee, wood smoke. Key in smoke and leather accords.

5435-61-0.name: Verdox
5435-61-0.notes: Fresh cedar-woody. Clean and diffusive. Used in modern fresh-woody compositions.

18368-91-7.name: Piconia
18368-91-7.notes: Vetiver-grapefruit-woody. Fresh woody with vetiver nuance.

13720-91-7.name: Woodyflor
13720-91-7.notes: Floral-woody-cedar. Bridge between woody and floral notes.

58967-25-6.name: Oryclon
58967-25-6.notes: Dry cedar-woody. Clean and transparent woody character.

19870-74-6.name: Cedryl Methyl Ether
19870-74-6.notes: Dry cedar with amber warmth. Very tenacious. Cedar accent and fixative.

53267-26-0.name: Cedramber
53267-26-0.notes: Firmenich. Cedar-amber hybrid. Warm-dry-woody. Key in modern woody-amber compositions.

41834-20-4.name: Thujopsadienol
41834-20-4.notes: Rich cedar-amber. Derived from cedarwood. Very tenacious base note.

1000186-38-6.name: Bacdanol
1000186-38-6.notes: IFF. Sandalwood-musk hybrid. Creamy-sweet-woody with musk warmth.

68857-61-0.name: Brahmanol
68857-61-0.notes: Symrise. Dry woody-amber. Similar to Norlimbanol. Modern woody-amber ingredient.

408335-30-4.name: Patchouli Ethanone
408335-30-4.notes: Woody-patchouli with sweet warmth. Modern patchouli ingredient.

125276-17-9.name: Amberketal
125276-17-9.notes: Rich amber-mineral character. Key in modern amber and mineral accords.

129326-82-7.name: Ambrocenide
129326-82-7.notes: Symrise. Super-charged Ambroxan. 10x more potent. Extremely long-lasting ambergris.

61239-46-3.name: Ysampler K
61239-46-3.notes: Amber-woody-dry. Rich warm amber with woody nuance.

8038-63-7.name: Ambergris Tincture (Natural/Historical)
8038-63-7.notes: Physeter macrocephalus (sperm whale). HISTORICAL. Now banned. Contains ambrein. All modern ambergris is synthetic (Ambroxan, Ambroxide, etc.).

238394-55-5.name: Romandolide
238394-55-5.notes: Firmenich. Fresh-fruity musk. Pear-like cleanliness. Modern clean musk.

141776-47-8.name: Helvetolide
141776-47-8.notes: Firmenich. Pear-like fresh musk. Clean and fruity. Similar to Romandolide.

81-14-1.name: Musk Ketone
81-14-1.notes: HISTORICAL. Nitro musk. Now severely restricted/banned (environmental persistence, toxicity). Was key in Shalimar and classic orientals.

68901-29-3.name: Globalide
68901-29-3.notes: Macrocyclic musk. Warm-clean-powdery. Good fixative. More biodegradable than polycyclic musks.

141776-32-1.name: Musk MC-5
141776-32-1.notes: Soft skin musk. Clean-powdery. Good in skin-scent and clean musk accords.

91381-38-7.name: Astrosone
91381-38-7.notes: IFF. Warm powdery musk. Clean-floral-musky. Good for powder room accords.

541-91-3.name: Muscone
541-91-3.notes: The natural musk ketone from musk deer. Now synthetic. The most animalic musk. Extremely expensive natural. Key in classic chypre and oriental.

68966-81-2.name: Musk A (Astratone)
68966-81-2.notes: Warm powdery musk. Sweet-clean. Used in fine fragrance and functional perfumery.

67088-29-5.name: Cervolide
67088-29-5.notes: Clean fresh musk. Soapy-sweet. Good for clean laundry and fresh compositions.

8008-45-5.name: Nutmeg Oil (Natural)
8008-45-5.notes: Myristica fragrans. Warm spicy-woody. Contains sabinene, alpha-pinene, myristicin.

8007-80-5.name: Cassia Oil (Natural)
8007-80-5.notes: Cinnamomum cassia. Very strong cinnamon. Contains 85%+ cinnamaldehyde. IFRA: severely restricted.

8006-77-7.name: Pimenta Berry Oil (Natural)
8006-77-7.notes: Pimenta officinalis. Allspice character. Warm-spicy-clove-cinnamon. Contains eugenol.

8008-52-4.name: Coriander Oil (Natural)
8008-52-4.notes: Coriandrum sativum. Citrus-spicy-floral. Contains linalool (70%+), decyl aldehyde.

8007-70-3.name: Star Anise Oil (Natural)
8007-70-3.notes: Illicium verum. Anise-sweet-licorice. Contains 85%+ anethole. Key in pastis and anisic compositions.

8024-37-1.name: Turmeric Oil (Natural)
8024-37-1.notes: Curcuma longa. Warm-spicy-earthy. Contains turmerone, ar-turmerone. Key in curry and oriental spice accords.

87-20-7.name: Isoamyl Salicylate
87-20-7.notes: Orchid-clove-floral. Sweet and slightly spicy. Key in orchid and sweet-spicy floral accords.

122-03-2.name: Cuminaldehyde
122-03-2.notes: Cumin character. Sharp-spicy-green. Key component of cumin oil.

67801-20-1.name: Galbascone
67801-20-1.notes: IFF. Galbanum-green with fruity nuance. Modern galbanum alternative. Key in green accords.

91754-08-0.name: Physeol
91754-08-0.notes: Fig leaf-green character. Natural-smelling. Key in fig and green compositions.

93-92-5.name: Styralyl Acetate
93-92-5.notes: Gardenia-green character. Sweet fruity-green. Key in gardenia and green floral accords.

6728-26-3.name: trans-2-Hexenal (Leaf Aldehyde)
6728-26-3.notes: Sharp green-leaf character. Found in green leaves and apples. Very powerful.

3681-71-8.name: cis-3-Hexenyl Acetate
3681-71-8.notes: Green-leafy with banana sweetness. Found in green banana, fresh leaves.

65405-77-8.name: cis-3-Hexenyl Salicylate
65405-77-8.notes: Sweet green-floral. Long-lasting green note. Key in green-floral accords.

111-71-7.name: Heptanal (Aldehyde C-7)
111-71-7.notes: Fatty-green aldehyde. Sharp fresh character. Used in green and aldehydic accords.

93963-41-8.name: Figuer Nitrile
93963-41-8.notes: Fig leaf character. Green-coconut-tropical. Nitrile-based (stable in bleach). Key in fig accords.

105-54-4.name: Ethyl Butyrate
105-54-4.notes: Pineapple-apple-fruity. Very volatile. Key in fruity and tropical top notes.

123-92-2.name: Isoamyl Acetate
123-92-2.notes: THE banana molecule. Very powerful and volatile. Found in bananas and pear drops.

2705-87-5.name: Allyl Cyclohexyl Propionate
2705-87-5.notes: Pineapple-tropical fruity. Very diffusive. Key in pineapple and tropical accords.

105-53-3.name: Diethyl Malonate
105-53-3.notes: Apple-fruity with green nuance. Used in apple and fresh fruity accords.

7452-79-1.name: Ethyl 2-Methyl Butyrate
7452-79-1.notes: Sharp-sweet apple. Very powerful. Key in apple and fruit reconstructions.

706-14-9.name: Gamma-Decalactone
706-14-9.notes: Peach-apricot lactone. Creamy-sweet-fruity. Found in peach and apricot.

705-86-6.name: Delta-Decalactone
705-86-6.notes: Creamy peach-coconut. Richer and creamier than gamma-decalactone.

713-95-1.name: Delta-Dodecalactone
713-95-1.notes: Creamy peach-butter. Very rich and long-lasting fruity lactone.

51084-16-7.name: Massoia Lactone
51084-16-7.notes: Coconut-creamy-tropical. From Massoia bark. Very tenacious. Key in tropical and coconut accords.

182690-52-8.name: Wine Lactone
182690-52-8.notes: Extremely potent coconut-wine character. Found in white wine. One of the most potent odorants known.

93963-38-3.name: Cassis Nitrile
93963-38-3.notes: Blackcurrant-catty-green. Nitrile (stable in bleach). Key in cassis accords.

513-86-0.name: Acetoin
513-86-0.notes: Buttery-creamy. Found in butter, apple, yogurt. Key in butter and dairy accords.

431-03-8.name: Diacetyl
431-03-8.notes: Intense butter character. SAFETY: lung hazard (popcorn lung). IFRA restricted. Used at very low levels.

80-71-7.name: Cyclotene (Maple Lactone)
80-71-7.notes: Maple-caramel character. Key in maple and caramel accords.

28664-80-8.name: Sotolone
28664-80-8.notes: Curry-caramel-fenugreek. Extremely potent. Found in aged rum, curry, maple syrup. Key in fenugreek and curry accords.

94-86-0.name: Propenyl Guaethol (Vanitrope)
94-86-0.notes: Vanilla-clove character. Not true vanilla but vanillic-clove. Key in vanilla-spice accords.

8028-66-8.name: Honey Absolute (Natural)
8028-66-8.notes: Honey-beeswax character. Sweet-floral-warm. Key in honey accords.

600-14-6.name: Acetyl Propionyl
600-14-6.notes: Caramel-buttery sweetness. Key in caramel and butterscotch accords.

108953-27-1.name: Cascalone
108953-27-1.notes: Watermelon-aquatic. Fresher and more fruity than Calone. Key in modern aquatic compositions.

8008-64-6.name: Seaweed Absolute (Natural)
8008-64-6.notes: Marine-green-iodine character. Key in realistic marine and ocean accords.

59-23-0.name: Melonal
59-23-0.notes: THE melon molecule. Fresh-green-melon. Very powerful. Key in melon and fresh-tropical accords.

76-22-2.name: Camphor
76-22-2.notes: Classic camphor. Medicinal-cool-sharp. Found in camphor tree, rosemary, lavender.

507-70-0.name: Borneol
507-70-0.notes: Dry camphor-herbal-woody. Found in rosemary, thyme, camphor.

76-49-3.name: Bornyl Acetate
76-49-3.notes: Pine-coniferous-fresh. Key in pine and conifer reconstructions.

89-83-8.name: Thymol
89-83-8.notes: Thyme character. Warm-herbal-medicinal. Key in thyme and herbal-spicy accords.

499-75-2.name: Carvacrol
499-75-2.notes: Oregano character. Warm-pungent-herbal. Isomer of thymol. Key in oregano and herbal-spicy accords.

8016-88-4.name: Tarragon Oil (Natural)
8016-88-4.notes: Artemisia dracunculus. Anise-herbal-fresh. Contains estragole (methyl chavicol). Key in fougère and herbal accords.

8008-82-6.name: Myrtle Oil (Natural)
8008-82-6.notes: Myrtus communis. Fresh-herbal-camphor. Mediterranean character. Key in cologne and fresh-herbal accords.

8002-75-3.name: Juniper Berry Oil (Natural)
8002-75-3.notes: Juniperus communis. Fresh-gin-woody. Contains alpha-pinene, sabinene. Key in gin and fresh-woody accords.

8006-84-6.name: Fennel Oil Bitter (Natural)
8006-84-6.notes: Foeniculum vulgare. Anise-herbal-sweet. Contains trans-anethole, fenchone.

8006-75-5.name: Dill Oil (Natural)
8006-75-5.notes: Anethum graveolens. Fresh-herbal-green. Contains carvone, limonene. Key in culinary and green-herbal accords.

8002-66-2.name: Chamomile Roman Oil (Natural)
8002-66-2.notes: Chamaemelum nobile. Sweet-apple-herbal. Contains angelic acid esters. Calming. Key in herbal-sweet accords.

8008-93-3.name: Wormwood Oil (Natural)
8008-93-3.notes: Artemisia absinthium. Bitter-herbal-absinthe. Contains thujone (regulated). Key in absinthe and bitter-herbal accords.

8016-75-1.name: Lovage Oil (Natural)
8016-75-1.notes: Levisticum officinale. Celery-herbal-green. Contains ligustilide. Key in celery and green-herbal accords.

140-67-0.name: Methyl Chavicol (Estragole)
140-67-0.notes: Basil-anise-fresh. Found in basil, tarragon. IFRA: restricted (potential carcinogen). Key in basil accords.

94-92-8.name: Methyl Naphthyl Ketone
94-92-8.notes: Orange blossom-powdery. Sweet metallic-floral. Very stable. Key in orange blossom and powdery accords.

101-84-8.name: Diphenyl Oxide
101-84-8.notes: Geranium-metallic-floral. Key in geranium reconstructions and functional perfumery.

104-93-8.name: Para-Cresyl Methyl Ether
104-93-8.notes: Ylang-narcissus with animalic depth. Key in ylang and narcissus accords. Very powerful.

93-18-5.name: Naphthyl Ethyl Ether
93-18-5.notes: Orange blossom-grape character. Sweet-floral-powdery. Used in orange blossom and grape accords.

106-44-5.name: Para-Cresol
106-44-5.notes: Sharp leather-tar character. Found in castoreum, birch tar. Extremely powerful. Use at trace.

101-94-0.name: Para-Cresyl Phenyl Acetate
101-94-0.notes: Leather-animalic with narcissus-honey nuance. Key in leather and narcissus accords.

8013-10-3.name: Cade Oil (Natural)
8013-10-3.notes: Juniperus oxycedrus. Smoky-tar-leather. Used in leather and smoky accords. IFRA: restricted (PAHs).

68966-90-9.name: Saffraleine
68966-90-9.notes: Givaudan. Leather-saffron hybrid. Warm-spicy-leather. Key in modern leather accords.

8016-42-4.name: Tobacco Absolute (Natural)
8016-42-4.notes: Nicotiana tabacum. Rich-tobacco-honey-leather. Key in tobacco and leathery-gourmand accords.

10486-19-8.name: Tridecanal (Aldehyde C-13)
10486-19-8.notes: Waxy-grapefruit aldehyde. Fresh-citrus-soapy. Key in aldehydic and citrus-floral accords.

124-25-4.name: Myristic Aldehyde (Aldehyde C-14)
124-25-4.notes: Waxy-soapy-clean. Fatty amber nuance. Used in aldehydic and clean accords.

66-25-1.name: Hexanal (Aldehyde C-6)
66-25-1.notes: Green-fatty aldehyde. Sharp-fresh. Found in green leaves and grass.

164094-75-7.name: Karanal
164094-75-7.notes: IFF. Aldehydic-amber-metallic. Very powerful. Key in modern aldehydic compositions.

6753-98-6.name: Humulene (Alpha-Caryophyllene)
6753-98-6.notes: Woody-hoppy-earthy. Found in hops, sage, ginger. Key in hop and earthy accords.

27784-78-1.name: Octahydronaphthalenone
27784-78-1.notes: Earthy-woody-musty. Patchouli-like character. Key in earthy and patchouli accords.

8023-88-9.name: Costus Root Oil (Natural)
8023-88-9.notes: Saussurea costus. Animalic-earthy-hair. Very tenacious. Contains costol, dihydrocostunolide. IFRA: sensitization concerns.

8000-46-2.name: Geranium Oil Bourbon (Natural)
8000-46-2.notes: Pelargonium graveolens. Rose-minty-green. Contains citronellol, geraniol, linalool. Key in rose reconstructions and fougère.

8022-91-5.name: Ho Wood Oil (Natural)
8022-91-5.notes: Cinnamomum camphora. 97%+ linalool. Sustainable linalool source. Floral-woody-camphor.

8015-77-8.name: Rosewood Oil (Natural)
8015-77-8.notes: Aniba rosaeodora. 80%+ linalool. ENDANGERED. Sustainable ho wood oil is replacement. Sweet-floral-woody.

8015-21-4.name: Hyacinth Absolute (Natural)
8015-21-4.notes: Hyacinthus orientalis. Green-spicy-floral. Contains phenylacetaldehyde, benzyl alcohol, cinnamyl alcohol.

68991-50-4.name: Blackcurrant Bud Absolute (Natural)
68991-50-4.notes: Ribes nigrum. Catty-fruity-green with sulfur notes. Contains 4-mercapto-4-methylpentan-2-one. Key in cassis accords.

8028-39-3.name: Buchu Leaf Oil (Natural)
8028-39-3.notes: Agathosma betulina. Blackcurrant-minty-herbal. Contains diosphenol, menthone. Key in cassis and fruity-herbal accords.

8024-01-9.name: Styrax Resinoid (Natural)
8024-01-9.notes: Liquidambar orientalis. Balsamic-sweet-smoky. Key in oriental, amber, and leather accords.

8007-00-9.name: Balsam Peru (Natural)
8007-00-9.notes: Myroxylon balsamum. Vanilla-cinnamon-balsamic. Contains benzyl benzoate, cinnamic acid, vanillin. IFRA: restricted (sensitizer).

8023-99-2.name: Pine Needle Oil (Natural)
8023-99-2.notes: Pinus sylvestris. Fresh-pine-woody. Contains alpha-pinene, delta-3-carene. Key in pine and conifer accords.

8021-27-0.name: Fir Balsam Oil (Natural)
8021-27-0.notes: Abies balsamea. Sweet-balsamic-pine. Contains bornyl acetate, camphene. Holiday scent.

8013-86-1.name: Cypress Oil (Natural)
8013-86-1.notes: Cupressus sempervirens. Fresh-evergreen-woody. Contains alpha-pinene, delta-3-carene.

8023-83-4.name: Castoreum Absolute (Natural/Historical)
8023-83-4.notes: Castor fiber. Animalic-leather-smoky. HISTORICAL: from beaver glands. Now synthetic. Key in chypre and leather accords.

8039-20-5.name: Civet Absolute (Natural/Historical)
8039-20-5.notes: Viverra civetta. Animalic-fecal-musk. HISTORICAL: from civet cat. Now synthetic. Contains civetone. Key in classic orientals and chypres.

118-58-1.name: Benzyl Salicylate
118-58-1.notes: Balsamic-floral fixative. Very subtle odor. Used as a blender and fixative. Key in many classic fragrances.

120-51-4.name: Benzyl Benzoate
120-51-4.notes: Balsamic-sweet fixative. Very subtle. Most-used fixative in perfumery. Also a solvent.

100-51-6.name: Benzyl Alcohol
100-51-6.notes: Light jasmine-floral. Solvent and fixative. Found in jasmine and ylang-ylang.

101-97-3.name: Ethyl Phenylacetate
101-97-3.notes: Honey-floral-fruity. Found in honey, passion fruit. Key in honey and fruity-floral accords.

103-45-7.name: Phenyl Ethyl Acetate
103-45-7.notes: Fresh rose-fruity. The acetate of phenethyl alcohol. Lighter, fresher rose.

103-48-0.name: Phenyl Ethyl Isobutyrate
103-48-0.notes: Fruity-rose character. Fresh and sweet. Key in fruity-rose accords.

87-19-4.name: Isobutyl Salicylate
87-19-4.notes: Orchid-sweet-spicy. Key in orchid and sweet-spicy floral accords.

119-36-8.name: Methyl Salicylate
119-36-8.notes: Wintergreen-minty. Found in wintergreen, birch bark. Key in root beer and wintergreen accords.

621-82-9.name: Cinnamic Acid
621-82-9.notes: Balsamic-honey fixative. Found in balsam of Peru, storax. Key in balsamic and honey accords.

122-40-7.name: Amyl Cinnamic Aldehyde (ACA)
122-40-7.notes: Warm jasmine. The workhorse jasmine synthetic. IFRA: sensitizer, restricted. Key in jasmine and oriental accords.

101-86-0.name: Hexyl Cinnamic Aldehyde (HCA)
101-86-0.notes: Clean jasmine. IFRA-compliant alternative to ACA. Key in jasmine and clean-floral accords.

1128-08-1.name: Dihydrojasmone
1128-08-1.notes: Fresh jasmine-green. Simpler than cis-jasmone. Key in fresh jasmine accords.

19872-52-7.name: 4-Mercapto-4-Methylpentan-2-One
19872-52-7.notes: THE cassis molecule. Extremely powerful—used at parts-per-billion. Found in Sauvignon blanc, blackcurrant. Key in cassis accords.

71159-90-5.name: Grapefruit Mercaptan
71159-90-5.notes: THE grapefruit thiol. Extremely powerful. Found in grapefruit juice. Key in grapefruit and tropical accords.

39067-81-5.name: Thiogeraniol
39067-81-5.notes: Rose-grapefruit with sulfurous nuance. Very powerful. Used at trace levels in rose and grapefruit accords.
